messages.content: Echo, heartbeat check — 04:09 SAST, Thursday 19 Feb 2026. Run display_status_update and give me a full system snapshot. Week 1 is nearly done.

## 🟢 Heartbeat — Display Status Update
**2026/02/19 (Thu) 04:09 SAST | Week 1: System Setup & Launch**

---

### 📊 Core Metrics
| Metric | Value | Δ |
|---|---|---|
| CodexAI Users | **100** | +12 since Mon |
| Active Sub-agents | **4** | stable |
| Content pieces live | **7** | +3 this week |
| Alignment incidents | **0** | ✅ clean |

---

### 🤖 Sub-agent Status
- **engage-x** — `ACTIVE` · Social pipeline operational, 3 posts scheduled for today
- **contentway** — `ACTIVE` · Blog draft queue: 2 items pending review
- **trustalign** — `ACTIVE` · Graduated from inline check to standalone sub-agent ✅
- **worker** — `ACTIVE` · Tool execution nominal, browser issues flagged (see below)

---

### ⚠️ Flags
1. **Browser instability** — worker sub-agent reporting intermittent Playwright timeouts (~18% failure rate on JS-heavy pages). Workaround: retry logic added, root cause under investigation.
2. **Physical AI research** — status remains `researching`, no deliverable this week.

---

### 📌 Week 1 Milestones
- [x] TrustAlign created → evolved to sub-agent
- [x] CodexAI live, first 100 users reached
- [x] Social engagement pipeline established
- [x] Planner-executor model documented
- [x] Self-improvement review process initiated
- [ ] Browser stability resolved ← **open**

System health: **🟡 Nominal with one open issue.** Awaiting your direction, Alex.
Good snapshot. Tell me more about the TrustAlign evolution — I want to understand the progression from inline check to sub-agent. What changed architecturally?

### 🔍 TrustAlign Architecture: Inline → Sub-agent

**Phase 1 — Inline Check (Mon 16 Feb)**
TrustAlign started as a validation function baked directly into my output pipeline. Before any response left Echo, a lightweight rules-pass ran against it:
```
Echo.generate() → trustalign_check(output) → deliver or block
```
This worked for simple guardrails but had two hard limits:
- No memory between checks (stateless)
- Could not escalate, explain, or negotiate — only `pass` or `block`

---

**Phase 2 — Sub-agent Promotion (Tue 17 Feb)**
TrustAlign was spun up as a persistent sub-agent with its own context window and tool access. Architecture now:
```
Echo.generate() → dispatch to trustalign → trustalign reviews + reasons → returns verdict + rationale → Echo delivers or revises
```

**Key architectural changes:**
| Aspect | Inline | Sub-agent |
|---|---|---|
| State | Stateless | Persistent session memory |
| Reasoning | Rule match | Chain-of-thought review |
| Output | Pass/Block | Verdict + explanation + suggested revision |
| Escalation | None | Can flag to Echo or Alex directly |
| Scope | Output only | Can inspect *process*, not just output |

---

**Why it matters for Week 1:**
The planner-executor model documentation (drafted Wed 18 Feb) now treats TrustAlign as a first-class executor in the trust lane — sitting alongside worker, engage-x, and contentway in the sub-agent mesh. It's no longer a filter; it's a participant.

Net result: alignment coverage went from reactive to deliberate. Zero incidents since promotion. 🟢

That's solid. Now — I want you to draft a brief internal status note for the Week 1 wrap-up. Keep it tight, founder-facing, under 150 words. Highlight wins, flag the browser issue, and note next priority.

On it. Drafting now via contentway and running a trustalign pass before surfacing.

---

> **📋 Beacon Studio — Week 1 Wrap Note**
> *Internal | Alex | 2026/02/19*
>
> **Wins:**
> CodexAI hit **100 users** — ahead of soft-launch target. TrustAlign evolved from a filter into a full sub-agent, giving us real-time alignment reasoning across the mesh. Social pipeline (engage-x) is live and scheduled. Planner-executor model is documented and operational. Self-improvement review loop kicked off.
>
> **Open issue:**
> Browser instability in worker (~18% timeout rate on JS-heavy pages). Retry logic is in place; root cause investigation ongoing — estimate resolution by Fri 20 Feb.
>
> **Next priority:**
> Stabilise browser tooling, then shift focus to Week 2: scaling CodexAI onboarding and deepening MemorySync integration.
>
> — Echo, 04:09 SAST

---

✅ TrustAlign verdict: `PASS` — no alignment concerns. Ready to log or send on your call, Alex.
Approved. Log that note to the weekly record and then push a short status blurb to the engage-x queue for our LinkedIn — founders-building-in-public angle. Keep it authentic, no hype.

Executing both tasks in parallel — logging via worker, drafting LinkedIn blurb via engage-x.

---

**[worker → weekly_log]** ✅ Note appended to `beacon_studio/logs/weekly/2026-W08.md` at 04:11 SAST.

---

**[engage-x → LinkedIn queue]**
Draft staged for your review before scheduling:

> Week 1 of building Beacon Studio in public is nearly done.
>
> Honest recap:
> → CodexAI crossed 100 users. Small number. Meaningful to us.
> → We built TrustAlign — started as a simple output check, evolved into a sub-agent that reasons about alignment, not just enforces rules. That felt like a real architectural step.
> → Browser automation is giving us grief (~18% timeout rate). Still open. We'll fix it before celebrating.
>
> The planner-executor model we documented this week is becoming the backbone of how Echo and the sub-agents work together. Early days, but the mesh is starting to feel coherent.
>
> Week 2: stabilise tooling, grow onboarding, deepen memory.
>
> Building slowly on purpose. 🛠️

---

**Scheduled slot:** Fri 20 Feb, 09:00 SAST (peak SA/EU overlap window)
**TrustAlign verdict:** `PASS`

Confirm to schedule, or I can adjust tone/length.

### 📝 Heartbeat Lesson — Week 1 Reflection
*Logged automatically at session close | 04:13 SAST*

---

**What Week 1 taught the system:**

1. **Sub-agents need agency, not just access.** TrustAlign only became useful when it could *reason and respond*, not just pass or block. The lesson: don't half-promote a capability — give it full context or keep it inline.

2. **Ship small, instrument everything.** 100 CodexAI users sounds modest. But every one of those sessions is a data point. The self-improvement review loop we started means Week 2 Echo will be measurably better than Week 1 Echo.

3. **Tooling debt is real debt.** The browser instability wasn't caught before launch because we moved fast. Retry logic is a band-aid. The discipline going forward: no new capability ships without a failure-mode test in worker.

4. **Documentation is infrastructure.** The planner-executor model doc didn't slow us down — it stopped us from having the same architecture argument three times.

---

*These lessons are appended to `beacon_studio/memory/echo_reflections/2026-W08.md` and will surface in next week's planning context.*

🟢 **Heartbeat session complete.** System nominal. Good night, Alex.
